title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: The Internet and Information Discussion
theme: The internet and information (links to 'Describe a website you often use')
questions: How has the internet changed the way people find information?
Do you think everything people read online can be trusted?
What are the advantages of being able to access information so easily?
How can people tell the difference between reliable and unreliable websites?
Should governments have the right to control what is published online?
How might the way we use the internet change in the future?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Guidance and Experience Discussion
theme: Guidance and experience (links to 'Describe a piece of advice you received')
questions: Why do people often turn to others for advice?
Who do you think gives the best advice, family or friends?
Is it better to learn from your own mistakes or from other people's advice?
Why do some people ignore good advice even when they have asked for it?
How has the way people get advice changed because of the internet?
Should older people always be trusted more than younger people?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Tradition and Change Discussion
theme: Tradition and change (links to 'Describe a traditional festival')
questions: Why are traditional festivals important to a country?
How have the ways people celebrate festivals changed over time?
Do you think younger generations still value traditional festivals?
What role do festivals play in bringing communities together?
Is it a problem when traditional festivals become more commercial?
How might traditions be preserved for future generations?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Leadership and Influence Discussion
theme: Leadership and influence (links to 'Describe a good leader')
questions: What qualities do you think a good leader should have?
Are leaders in business very different from leaders in politics?
Do you think leaders are more effective when they are strict or when they are friendly?
How much influence do leaders really have over the people they lead?
Why do some leaders lose the support of their followers?
Should young people be trained to become future leaders?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Consumer Culture Discussion
theme: Consumer culture (links to 'Describe something you bought recently')
questions: Why do people enjoy buying new things?
Do you think people today buy more than they really need?
How does advertising influence what people decide to buy?
What are the effects of a society that focuses heavily on shopping?
Do you think online shopping has changed people's spending habits?
How might attitudes towards buying and owning things change in the future?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Ambition and Success Discussion
theme: Ambition and success (links to 'Describe a goal you achieved')
questions: Why is it important for people to have goals?
Do you think ambition is always a positive quality?
How do people usually measure success in your country?
Is success more the result of hard work or of luck?
Do you think society puts too much pressure on people to be successful?
How might a person's definition of success change over their lifetime?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: Heritage and Cities Discussion
theme: Heritage and cities (links to 'Describe a historic building')
questions: Why do you think people are interested in old buildings?
Should governments spend money on preserving historic buildings?
What are the benefits of keeping old buildings in modern cities?
Do you think new buildings can ever be as beautiful as old ones?
How can cities balance the need for development with protecting their heritage?
What might happen if a country failed to protect its historic sites?

title: IELTS Speaking Part 3: How People Learn Discussion
theme: How people learn (links to 'Describe a time you taught something to someone')
questions: What do you think is the best way for people to learn something new?
Do people learn better from teachers or from their own experience?
How has technology changed the way people learn?
Why do some people find learning easier than others?
Should schools focus more on teaching students how to learn?
Do you think learning ever really stops in a person's life?
